_schema.description: Manual trading-name <-> FHRSID alias table for Stratford trial. Each entry anchors a legal-entity or composite FSA record to a consumer-facing trading name. The resolver applies these + attaches the consumer-facing name as `public_name` and any other known trading names as `trading_names` so name-based search reaches the right record.
_schema.scope: Stratford trial. Reviewed manually against stratford_establishments.json (210 records).

aliases.source: FSA name field 'The Townhouse Also Trading As JR Hotel And Restaurants LTD'
FSA 'The Crofts Cafe Also Trading As New York Pizzas'
FSA 'Bella Italia Also Trading As Super Nonna'
Exact FSA name match (pre-existing Rankable-A venue)
Address '9 Church Street'; supersedes the venue historically known as 'No.9 Church Street'
aliases.note: No.9 Church Street closed; Pick Thai now occupies 9 Church Street. Customer searches for 'No.9' should surface this record with the historical-occupancy caveat, not an absence.

ambiguous_gpids.site: Welcome Break Warwick Services Southbound, M40
ambiguous_gpids.reason_for_operator: Two separate brands share the service-station forecourt. Google Places returns one listing for the whole site; the FSA records each brand separately. Until each brand has a distinct Google Business Profile, both venues are flagged ambiguous and remain in Directional-C.
ambiguous_gpids.resolution_path: Each brand operator requests a distinct Google Business Profile for their unit (franchise head office typically handles this for chain brands).
ambiguous_gpids.note: Two FSA records attached to the same Google Place (same service station unit). Mark both ambiguous.

ambiguous_gpids.reason_for_operator: Two FSA records at different postcodes share one Google Place. Most commonly this is a rebrand or a relocation where the Google Business Profile survived and the old FSA record was not retired. Without operator confirmation it is not safe to assume either is closed.
ambiguous_gpids.resolution_path: Operator confirms whether 'The Margin' or 'The Water Margin @ The Hollybush' is the current trading entity. Once confirmed, set `fsa_closed = true` on the retired record (via a manual patch on the establishments file) and remove the retired FHRSID from this entry.
ambiguous_gpids.note: Two FSA records pointing at the same Google Place at different postcodes. Likely a rebrand or a Google matching error; mark both ambiguous until disambiguated.

ambiguous_gpids.site: Jaguar Land Rover, Gaydon Test Centre, Banbury Road, Gaydon
ambiguous_gpids.reason_for_operator: Three separately FSA-registered food units inside a single private industrial site (JLR Gaydon Test Centre). Google Places has one listing for the site as a whole, so all three FHRS records resolve to the same Google Place ID. Public ranking is not the right surface for private-site canteens; Directional-C is the correct permanent class here.
ambiguous_gpids.resolution_path: These records will likely remain Directional-C permanently. If distinct Google listings exist for each unit, the alias table can be updated per unit; otherwise accept the Directional-C classification as the honest output.
ambiguous_gpids.note: Three FSA records at a single MoD / industrial site sharing one Google Place. All ambiguous; Directional-C is the correct permanent state.

ambiguous_gpids.reason_for_operator: Two separately FSA-registered units inside the Bell Court food hall share one Google Place ID (likely the hall's shared listing). Until each unit has a distinct Google Business Profile, both remain ambiguous and land in Directional-C.
ambiguous_gpids.resolution_path: Each Bell Court unit requests a distinct Google Business Profile. Operators of multi-tenant food halls can do this via Google Business Profile Help.
ambiguous_gpids.note: Two Bell Court units sharing one Google Place. Ambiguous.

known_unresolved.note: High-profile venue. Not in the 210-record trial FSA slice. Consumer search must not silently return nothing; surface as 'not yet in trial dataset' with a link to feedback.
known_unresolved.unblock: Re-run .github/scripts/augment_fsa_stratford.py to pull all food business types for LA 320, or expand the trial slice.

known_unresolved.note: Operated by RSC Enterprises Ltd. Not in trial slice.
No FSA record plausibly maps to an 'Oscar's' trading name in Stratford. Either closed, never FSA-registered, or outside trial scope. Consumer search should return 'unknown venue'.
known_unresolved.unblock: Confirm existence via Google / TripAdvisor lookup; if still operational, add a manual FHRSID + expansion.
